title: Software Engineer Intern - Product development (Unpaid)
description:
Company Description
 
 Yuvro is building an AI\\-powered product ecosystem focused on transforming how students learn, practice, get assessed, and become industry\\-ready.
 



 We are building intelligent systems across learning, assessments, mock interviews, analytics, and scalable engineering products. At Yuvro, engineers are not just developers — they are builders who contribute ideas, solve real problems, and help shape innovative products.
 


 Internship Overview
 
 We are looking for
 **2027, 2028, 2029** 
 graduates with highly driven Software Engineering Interns passionate about coding, AI/ML, and product engineering. Interns will work on innovative systems like Practical Labs and advanced AI\\-powered hiring platforms while contributing to real product development. This internship is ideal for individuals who enjoy solving engineering challenges, thinking creatively, and building impactful technology with an entrepreneurial mindset.
 


 What You Will Work On
 * Backend engineering and scalable APIs
* AI/ML\\-integrated product workflows
* Practical Labs and intelligent assessment systems
* Advanced AI Hire and analytics platforms
* Product improvements, performance optimization, and innovation


 Skills \\& Technical Expectations
 * Strong understanding of Python / Java / C\\+\\+
* Data Structures \\& Algorithms fundamentals
* OOPs, DBMS, and System Design basics
* Problem\\-solving and logical thinking
* Basic understanding of AI/ML concepts
* Git/GitHub and backend development exposure


 What We Value
 * Entrepreneurial and builder mindset
* Ownership and proactive thinking
* Curiosity toward AI and product innovation
* Ability to contribute ideas along with development
* Strong learning attitude and engineering thinking


 What You Will Gain
 * Hands\\-on experience with production\\-level systems
* Exposure to real\\-world AI/ML product development
* Opportunity to work on innovative engineering products
* Offer Letter and Internship Completion Certificate
* Potential Stipend/full time opportunity based on performance

title: Senior Software Engineer (Java/AWS)
description:
**Business Unit:**
 Cubic Transportation Systems
   

  

**Company Details:**
 When you join Cubic, you become part of a company that creates and delivers technology solutions in transportation to make people’s lives easier by simplifying their daily journeys, and defense capabilities to help promote mission success and safety for those who serve their nation. Led by our talented teams around the world, Cubic is committed to solving global issues through innovation and service to our customers and partners.
   

  

 We have a top\\-tier portfolio of businesses, including Cubic Transportation Systems (CTS) and Cubic Defense (CD). Explore more on Cubic.com.
   

  

**Job Details:**
**Job Summary:** 
 We are seeking a Senior Software Engineer with strong experience in Java (Java 11\\+), Spring Boot, and microservices to design, build, and operate scalable, cloud\\-native applications on AWS. The role requires hands\\-on expertise across backend development, containers, CI/CD, and observability, along with a strong sense of ownership in production SaaS environments. You will collaborate with distributed teams, contribute to high\\-availability systems, and support customer\\-facing SLAs while following modern engineering, security, and Agile best practices.
   

  

**Essential Job Duties and Responsibilities:**
* Core stack: Strong Java (Java 11\\+), Spring Boot, microservices; proficiency with Bash/Python for automation and tooling.
* Cloud/AWS: Experience with EC2/ECS/EKS, Lambda, S3, RDS/DynamoDB, SQS/SNS, CloudWatch; foundational IAM knowledge.
* Containers \\& IaC: Docker; familiarity with Terraform or CloudFormation.
* CI/CD \\& Quality: Contribution to pipelines, testing (JUnit), code quality enforcement.
* Observability: Log/metric/trace usage; troubleshooting and performance optimization.
* Security Basics: Secure coding, secret management, vulnerability remediation.
* Collaboration: Agile practices, documentation, and code reviews.
* Operations: Participation in support rotation after ramp‑up.
* Nice to have: Messaging/streaming experience, Caching technologies (Hazelcast, Redis/ElastiCache ), API Gateway.


**Minimum Job Requirements:**
* Working US hours, roles with collaboration across distributed engineering teams.
* Bachelor's in CS/EE or another relevant discipline.
* Five (5\\)\\+ years of relevant professional experience.
* Strong ownership, problem\\-solving, and experience in production SaaS environments.
* Ability to support high‑availability systems and customer‑impacting SLAs.


**Worker Type:**
 Employee

title: Aws backend engineer
description:
Responsibilities:
 



 Legacy\\-to\\-Cloud Translation
 



 Interpret complex technical documentation of legacy mainframe systems to identify integration touchpoints and determine the downstream impacts of cloud migration
 



 Technical Advisory
 



 Evaluate and recommend optimal technical paths for modernizing integrations within the existing ecosystem, ensuring minimal disruption to active claims processing
 



 Architectural Alignment
 



 Collaborate closely with Enterprise Architects to ensure all solution designs strictly adhere to both the target\\-state cloud architecture and necessary "transitional\\-state" patterns
 



 Data Pipeline Engineering
 



 Design and optimize back\\-end data flows using cloud\\-native services, focusing on performance, scalability, and cost\\-efficiency
 



 Collaborative Design
 



 Participate in creative problem\\-solving sessions to bridge the gap between legacy data structures and modern cloud\\-native data environments
 



 Agile Participation
 



 Operate as an embedded member of the Health Plan Administration technical team, contributing to sprint cycles, daily stand\\-ups, and PI planning within a SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) environment
 



 Technical Communication
 



 Distill complex technical concepts into actionable insights for stakeholders to assist in the prioritization of backlog requirements and deadlines
 



 Qualifications:
 



 Technical Proficiency
 



 Strong experience with back\\-end development and data orchestration
 



 While the primary stack is AWS (Glue, S3, Lambda, Node.js), equivalent experience in other major cloud providers (Azure/GCP) will be considered
 



 Modernization Experience
 



 Familiar in working on large\\-scale migrations, specifically moving workloads or integrations from legacy/mainframe environments to the cloud
 



 Data Flow Optimization
 



 Strong understanding of how to manage and optimize high\\-volume data as it flows through serverless and microservices architectures
 



 Familiarity with ELT \\& ETL transformation concepts and relevant Data Quality practices
 



 Process Expertise
 



 Experience operating within a SAFe or Agile environment, with a clear understanding of how to manage changing priorities and shifting deadlines
 



 Soft Skills
 



 Exceptional communication skills with the ability to advocate for specific technical designs while remaining flexible to business needs
 







 Preferred Qualifications:
 



 Previous experience within the Health Insurance/Health Plan industry or claims processing domains
 



 Experience with automated testing and CI/CD pipelines in a cloud\\-native context
 



 A "consultative" mindset—the ability to act as a subject matter expert who can influence design decisions
 







 Additional Notes:
 



 The BCBS Association is going through the process of migrating their claims processing from their old mainframe tech to their new cloud environment. As they are going through this migration, Wellmark needs to understand how their integrations with their older tech can be modernized, while avoiding disruption in an environment of ever\\-changing deadlines and priorities.
 



 This Software Engineer would be responsible for interpreting technical documentation to determine impacts and make recommendations about which technical options are most appropriate for Wellmark’s ecosystem.
 



 This person will be required to work with architects to ensure solution designs align with Wellmark’s target\\-state cloud architecture, as well as transitional\\-state design patterns.
 



 Because most of this work will be centered around back\\-end data as it flows through (mostly) AWS cloud\\-native tech, experience with Glue, S3, Lambda, Node, data flow\\-optimization are strongly desired. (Equivalent skills from other stacks would qualify for consideration.)
 



 A successful candidate will be able to demonstrate creative problem\\-solving in the technical design space and an ability to clearly communicate complex concepts to assist in prioritization of technical requirements in a SAFe environment.
 



 They will be expected to operate as a member of an Agile team in Wellmark’s Health Plan Administration technical team.
 



 This has a possibility of transitioning into a full\\-time position.
 







 General Interview Tips:
 



  





 Connect with the Leader(s) on LinkedIn and/or review their profile prior to your meeting. Look for common connections, organizations you have in common and be sure to highlight any commonalities in your interview.
 



 Research the company prior to your meeting; demonstrate the knowledge you have of the company during the interview and express specific interest.
 



 Be outgoing and excited for the opportunity to interview; say why you want to work there.
 



 Mention any connections you have (if applicable) with Healthcare and Insurance (i.e. Former Blue Cross experience, Pharmaceutical, financial insurance, etc.)
 



 Have a list of questions for the interviewer(s) prepared. It’s good to have several as they will likely answer some of them during the course of the interview.
 



 At the end of the interview, ask something like, “Are there any questions I can answer that I haven’t already?”
 



  





 We need immediate joiner
 



  





 Share me your cv to kcharitha388@gmail.com

title: Lead Software Engineer II
description:
**Bring more to life.**
 Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? w
   

  

 At Cepheid, one of Danaher’s 15\\+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.
   

  

 You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.
   

  

 Here at Cepheid, we are building the future of healthcare from our cutting\\-edge R\\&D center in Bengaluru. Our diagnostic instruments are complex systems where precision, reliability, and safety are non\\-negotiable. At the core of these instruments is sophisticated firmware that must perform flawlessly. We are seeking a top\\-tier verification expert to serve as a guardian of our firmware quality and ensure it meets the highest standards of excellence.
   

  

 Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.
   

  

 We are seeking a
 **Lead Software Engineer II** 
 for our manufacturing software systems. This is a role focused on designing the software tools and infrastructure that are essential for producing our complex molecular diagnostic devices at scale.
   

  

 You will be responsible for designing and developing manufacturing software—from test automation frameworks and calibration routines to designing the databases that store critical production data. You will be the key technical bridge between R\\&D and Operations, translating complex product designs into robust, validated software solutions for the factory floor and field service teams.
   

  

 This position is part of the Software/ Firmware Engineering team located in Bengaluru, IDC, India and will be an on\\-site role.
   

  

**In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To**
* Develop, implement, and maintain manufacturing testing and calibration software applications using C\\# and C\\+\\+ with adherence to defined software development lifecycles, version control systems, and source control processes
* Diagnose and resolve manufacturing software and firmware test failures at contract manufacturing sites, including root cause analysis, debugging across MFG SW and FW layers, and driving measurable improvements in module build yield at CM
* Contribute to architecture design and implementation for next‑generation manufacturing software, including multi‑platform UI development, cloud server integration, multi‑layer back‑end logic development, data analysis, data acquisition, hardware interfacing and control, and GUI development
* Execute verification and validation activities for manufacturing software in collaboration with engineering, system verification, and SQA teams, including test method development and defect resolution workflows
* Support manufacturing operations by developing scalable software solutions, ensuring compatibility with hardware systems, and maintaining performance and reliability metrics across production environments


**The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field with 3\\+ years of professional experience (3\\+ years for MS and 5\\+ for BS) in software development, with a significant focus on manufacturing test, hardware\\-in\\-the\\-loop simulation, or test automation.
* Deep knowledge of in an object\\-oriented language such as C\\#/C\\+\\+, Java, Python.
* Experience with UI/WPF layout tools is a plus. strong development experience in TCP/IP, CAN, UDP and other network interfaces.
* Proven experience designing software that interfaces with and controls physical hardware and test equipment.
* Experience with SQL, XML, OOP/OOD


**It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:**
* Direct experience developing software under a diagnostic/ medical device quality management system (ISO 13485\\).
* Familiarity with the requirements for software validation in an FDA\\-regulated environment.
* Knowledge cloud program development is a plus.


 Cepheid, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job.
   

  

 Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info.
   

  

 At Cepheid we believe in designing a better, more sustainable workforce. We recognize the benefits of flexible, remote working arrangements for eligible roles and are committed to providing enriching careers, no matter the work arrangement. This position is eligible for a remote work arrangement in which you can work remotely from your home. Additional information about this remote work arrangement will be provided by your interview team. Explore the flexibility and challenge that working for Cepheid can provide.
   

  

 Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real\\-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.
   

  

 For more information, visit www.danaher.com.
